name: Google Drive Connector
description: Read and edit Google Docs, Google Sheets, and plain text files, and read Google Slides, stored in the user's Google Drive. Nothing is stored on Imperal — content is fetched live and written straight back to the source.

tools.name: skeleton_refresh_doc_reader_files
tools.description: Google Drive status — connected accounts, picked files, how many are indexed and ready, names, types

tools.name: connect_google_docs
tools.description: Start Google Drive OAuth — returns an authorisation URL to open in the browser. Connecting again adds another Google account (each keeps its own pool of picked files).

tools.name: open_file_picker
tools.description: Get a link to the Google Picker where the user selects which Drive files/folders Google Drive may access (for a specific connected account, default the active one). Picked items register automatically; call list_files afterwards.

account.description: Which connected Google account to pick files for (email). Omit to use the active account.
account.title: Account

tools.name: register_picked_files
tools.description: Manual fallback: register files from the JSON the Picker page showed, ONLY if it displayed a copy-paste box instead of confirming automatically (rare). Skips files already registered.

files.description: Files copied from the Google Picker page's output box (JSON with a 'files' array). Paste that exact array here.
files.title: Files

tools.name: list_files
tools.description: List the Drive files AND folders the user connected — name, type, size and indexing status (pending/indexing/ready/failed/cold). Folders can be opened with open_folder. Automatically picks up items just selected in the Picker. Does not return file content.

DocFile.description: A file the user picked (or a folder they granted) through the Picker.
DocFile.title: DocFile

tools.name: open_folder
tools.description: List the contents of a connected Google Drive folder (files + subfolders) by its folder_id from list_files. Read a file inside it with read_file.

folder_id.description: Google Drive folder ID (from list_files) whose contents to list.
folder_id.title: Folder Id

DocFile.description: A file the user picked (or a folder they granted) through the Picker.
DocFile.title: DocFile

return_schema.description: The children of one granted folder (drill-in view).
return_schema.title: FolderContents

tools.name: disconnect_file
tools.description: Remove a file from the connected list and delete its cached index (Postgres+Nextcloud). The file in Google Drive itself is untouched.

tools.name: list_accounts
tools.description: List the Google accounts connected to Google Drive — each account's email, which one is active, and how many files are in its own pool. Use when the user asks which Google accounts are connected or which one is in use.

AccountItem.description: A connected Google account and the size of its own picked-files pool.
AccountItem.title: AccountItem

tools.name: switch_account
tools.description: Change the active Google account. Subsequent file listing, picking, reading and editing use this account's own pool of picked files until switched again.

account.description: Which connected Google account — its email (from list_accounts).
account.title: Account

tools.name: disconnect_account
tools.description: Disconnect a Google account from Google Drive and forget its pool of picked files. Nothing is deleted in Google Drive itself; the OAuth grant can be fully revoked by the user at myaccount.google.com/permissions.

account.description: Which connected Google account — its email (from list_accounts).
account.title: Account

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read the text of ANY connected Drive file — Google Doc/Sheet/Slides or PDF/PPTX/DOCX/XLSX/plain text. Returns a windowed slice (character offset/limit). For a long file, prefer search_files to jump to the relevant part instead of reading it all.

file_id.description: Google Drive file ID (from list_files). Any type — Doc, Sheet, Slides, PDF, PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, text.
file_id.title: File Id

offset.description: 0-based character offset to start reading from.
offset.title: Offset

limit.description: Max characters to return. Omit for a sensible default window (use offset to page a long file, or search_files to jump to the relevant part).
limit.title: Limit

return_schema.description: A windowed, character-addressed slice of any file's extracted text.
return_schema.title: FileText

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Search the user's connected Drive files. Omit file_id to search across ALL files by meaning (semantic — returns the most relevant snippets, token-cheap). Give file_id to find an exact substring within ONE file. Use this instead of read_file when you only need to locate something.

file_id.description: Optional: restrict to ONE file (exact substring search in that file). Omit to search across ALL your files by meaning (semantic).
file_id.title: File Id

tools.name: file_overview
tools.description: A quick, cheap look at one file — its type, size, indexing status and a short preview — without reading the whole thing. Use to answer 'what is this file' before deciding to read it.

tools.name: edit_document
tools.description: Edit a Google Doc: op=replace (exact find-and-replace — fails if find_text has no match), op=append (add text to the end), or op=overwrite (replace the whole document). Changes the live document immediately.

op.description: replace = find-and-replace exact text; append = add to the end; overwrite = replace the whole document.
op.title: Op

find_text.description: For op=replace: exact text to find.
find_text.title: Find Text

replace_text.description: For op=replace: text to replace it with.
replace_text.title: Replace Text

match_case.description: For op=replace: require exact case match.
match_case.title: Match Case

text.description: For op=append: text to add at the end.
text.title: Text

content.description: For op=overwrite: the new full document content.
content.title: Content

tools.name: edit_spreadsheet
tools.description: Write a 2D array of values into a Google Sheet range (A1 notation, e.g. 'Sheet1!A1:D10'). Overwrites only that range on the live sheet.

values.description: Row-major 2D array of cell values to write into the range.
values.title: Values

tools.name: spreadsheet_compute
tools.description: Compute an EXACT sum/count/average/min/max over a Google Sheet range (A1 notation), calculated in code — not estimated from a text dump. Use for totals instead of reading raw cells.

operation.description: Exact computation over the range (count counts all non-empty cells; the rest use numeric cells).
operation.title: Operation

tools.name: write_text_file
tools.description: Overwrite a genuinely text-based Drive file (text/JSON/XML/YAML) with new content. Binary formats (PDF/DOCX/XLSX/PPTX) are read-only and will be refused.

file_id.description: File ID of a genuinely text-based file (text/JSON/XML/YAML). Binary formats (PDF/DOCX/etc) are read-only.
file_id.title: File Id

content.description: New full content — replaces everything currently in the file.
content.title: Content

tools.name: index_files
tools.description: Index the user's picked Drive files (extract text + build the search index) so they can be read and searched. Runs in the background — you'll get a message when it's done. Call after picking new files, or if a file reports it's still being prepared.

secrets.name: google_client_id
secrets.description: Shared Google OAuth Client ID for Google Drive (developer-owned; one OAuth app for all users)

secrets.name: google_client_secret
secrets.description: Shared Google OAuth Client Secret for Google Drive (developer-owned)

secrets.name: google_picker_api_key
secrets.description: Google API Key restricted to Picker API for the picker.html static page (HTTP referrer restricted)

secrets.name: picker_hmac_secret
secrets.description: HMAC key shared with doc-extractor-service for the Picker OAuth token handoff
